gaps: CF-gated HTML routes (post detail, comments, historical leaderboards, topic feeds, user profiles, collections, newsletter archive) are shipped as explicit stubs with honest messaging.
Write actions (upvote, comment, follow) require PRODUCT_HUNT_TOKEN and are out of scope for this token-free build.

novel_features.name: First-seen trajectory
novel_features.description: See when a product first appeared in the Product Hunt feed and how many days it lingered there.
novel_features.rationale: Product Hunt's own UI shows current day rank but hides historical presence; a local /feed snapshot store makes this queryable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need to judge a product's PH momentum without a token or the web UI.

novel_features.name: Launch-calendar
novel_features.description: Week-at-a-glance showing which products were featured each day.
novel_features.rationale: Requires multiple daily snapshots of the /feed joined by first-seen date; no single API call yields this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for retrospectives, competitive scans, or weekly maker newsletters.

novel_features.name: Maker burn chart
novel_features.description: Top authors (makers or hunters) across all synced /feed snapshots in a time window.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the Atom author field across every snapshot — a single /feed snapshot only surfaces that day's 50 authors.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when scouting prolific makers or writing a monthly recap.

novel_features.name: Outbound-URL drift
novel_features.description: Detect products whose external landing URL changed between sync cycles.
novel_features.rationale: Each /feed entry's canonical link hops through PH's /r/p/<id> redirect; comparing across snapshots surfaces beta→launch domain moves.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when tracking a product's web presence evolution over time.

novel_features.name: Tagline grep
novel_features.description: Regex/FTS search across every tagline the CLI has ever seen.
novel_features.rationale: /feed taglines are dense, high-signal one-liners; an FTS-backed archive over time turns them into a semantic filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this instead of grepping the PH website, which won't let automated clients read it.

novel_features.name: New-since-last-sync watcher
novel_features.description: Show only entries that appeared since the last sync. Idempotent; cron-friendly.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the last snapshot to diff against; /feed alone can't tell you what's new to you.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Wire this into a schedule or chat bot to get alerted on new launches without burning rate.

novel_features.name: Author-co-occurrence graph
novel_features.description: Authors who repeatedly appear alongside a given author in the same /feed snapshots.
novel_features.rationale: A rough social signal derived purely from the Atom author field across many snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for scouting hunter networks and co-launch patterns.

novel_features.name: Atom-first doctor
novel_features.description: Probe /feed, parse the Atom XML, validate store schema, and surface exact failure modes.
novel_features.rationale: This CLI's reachability story is unusual (only /feed works); the doctor has to explain the CF gate on HTML routes and the /feed health in one place.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a command returns 0 results, run doctor first to tell mass-failure from empty-delta.

narrative.headline: Product Hunt without the OAuth dance — watch today's launches, keep your own history, compose views PH itself doesn't expose.
narrative.value_prop: Every other Product Hunt client requires OAuth plus a paltry complexity budget. This one reads the public /feed Atom, persists it to local SQLite, and composes trend/calendar/maker-burn views the PH website hides. Token-free. Offline search. Agent-native on every command.
narrative.auth_narrative: No credentials needed — the CLI uses the public /feed. A future `auth login --chrome` pass can import Cloudflare clearance from your browser to unlock CF-gated HTML routes, but nothing in this build requires it.

quickstart.comment: Pull today's /feed into your local store. First run bootstraps everything.
Agent-friendly shortlist with exactly the fields you need.
See when a slug first showed up and how long it lingered.
Diff the live feed against your last sync; wire into cron for alerts.
Search every tagline you've ever synced by regex.

troubleshoots.symptom: Commands return empty results after install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `producthunt-pp-cli sync` first — the store starts empty; the CLI won't fabricate.
HTML routes on producthunt.com are blocked by Cloudflare for automated clients. Use `info <slug>` (backed by /feed) and `open <slug>` to launch the real page in a browser.
Run `doctor --json` to confirm /feed is serving Atom XML; PH occasionally returns a 503 during deploys.
Run `sync` a few times over several days — FTS indexes only what's been synced. A single snapshot is 50 entries.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when you want a terminal-first view of Product Hunt today: what launched, who launched it, which external URL it points to, and — uniquely — how the feed evolves over time. It is ideal for agents composing recaps, makers triaging their own feed coverage, and scouts wanting a token-free nightly cron.

recipes.title: Daily agent briefing
recipes.explanation: Sync first, then hand the agent a narrow, dotted-path payload — no unused JSON bloat.

recipes.title: Weekly maker recap
recipes.explanation: Aggregate the last 7 days of snapshots to surface the makers with the most features.

recipes.title: Tagline trend check
recipes.explanation: Pipe a broad regex against 90 days of taglines; dotted --select keeps the output tight.

recipes.title: Export scraper-parity CSV
recipes.explanation: Reproduces the fernandod1/ProductHunt-scraper column set without the browser scrape.

recipes.title: New-launch watcher
recipes.explanation: Diff-on-last-sync command designed for cron and agent use; idempotent if run at the same rate.
